title: Ceramic Avian Figurines on Wooden Shelving in a Buenos Aires Apartment Capital Federal Home
description: Buenos Aires, Capital Federal: Two intricately crafted ceramic bird sculptures rest elegantly upon the polished wooden shelves of a traditional Argentine household cabinet. The lower figurine, a vibrantly painted kingfisher, perches gracefully on a faux-branch, its sharp beak and detailed plumage captured with exquisite precision under soft, ambient indoor lighting. Directly above, a mallard duck displays a more subdued palette, its realistic feather textures contrasting against the smooth, warm-toned veneer of the mahogany-stained wood. The shallow depth of field isolates these delicate artifacts, emphasizing the quiet stillness that often defines personal living spaces amidst the frenetic energy of Argentina's sprawling metropolis. Such ornamental displays serve as subtle markers of domestic history and aesthetic refinement, reflecting the deep-seated Latin American tradition of curating meaningful objects within the sanctuary of the family home. Each piece reflects a commitment to traditional craftsmanship, bridging the gap between historical artisanal techniques and contemporary interior decor styles found throughout the city. Observing these curated items allows one to appreciate the intimate layer of daily life that exists behind the heavy wooden doors of urban residential buildings. These figures act as silent witnesses to the evolving cultural narrative of a city known for its eclectic mix of European influence and local artistic flair. Capturing these small details provides essential insight into the private heritage and regional identity that shape the character of the Argentine capital. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
